subject: Make base image name a property
commit_message:
Make base image name a property

The base image name is not configurable for Policy builds. This change
makes the base image name configurable so it can be reconfigured if
needs be.

Issue-ID: POLICY-2971
Change-Id: I8ba39aa109c5b4ddb516c27f65bf20dc1020d220
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>

Patch Set 2:

The FROM in pap,api, etc .. Dockerfiles will need to change to accommodate variable base image names, what's the plan for those components with regards to this change?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> The FROM in pap,api, etc .. Dockerfiles will need to change to accommodate variable base image names, what's the plan for those components with regards to this change?

Hi,

We have a requirement to make proprietary builds of some selected ONAP components. These images are to be as close as possible to the opensource images but they are based on company specific base images, this is many to allow compliance with Company specific product rules. We have set up a build system that inserts the required Dockerfiles at the correct stage of the build so that we can use the proprietary base images. In order to stay as close as possible to the opensource we don't want to change the pom files in a proprietary way as that will put us on a branch that risks unneeded divergence. So this change allows us to build with our different images in a controlled way with minimal drift from the open source.

Let me know if you want more information.
/Andrew

Thanks Andrew, I guess I don't see it very clear how you'll make it to work without changing the API, PAP .. Dockerfiles themselves but I am ok with the change.

Sorry for the bad explanation.

When we are building our proprietary builds just before calling mvn ... we run a script that replaces the existing Dockefiles with our proprietary ones. Then we call the existing mvn build which is why we don't want to start changing the POM file and just be able to control the necessary aspects by parameter.
